meta.description: Kingdom OS structured threat registry. Machine-readable, PEACE-integrated.

threats.name: ISP traffic metadata logging
mitigations: TLS on all connections hides content
4G router separates Kingdom ops from home WiFi
threats.residual_risk: ISP sees traffic volume and timing patterns even with TLS. Correlation of traffic bursts could reveal operational cadence.
threats.detection: Cannot detect ISP-side logging; assume it happens. Monitor for legal requests via canary warrant.
threats.response: If subpoena suspected: rotate exit nodes, shift to mobile-only operation, engage legal counsel.

threats.name: IP correlation across services
mitigations: Network compartments: home WiFi, 4G, VPS exits
Per-activity network assignment enforced via policy
threats.residual_risk: Timing correlation between 4G router traffic and VPS exit traffic could link identities for a sophisticated adversary.
threats.detection: Periodic review of egress IP consistency via vpn-route.sh status. PEACE fleet canary checks.
threats.response: If correlation detected: immediately switch VPS exit node, rotate WireGuard keys, review activity logs for exposure window.

threats.name: GitHub/API provider subpoena
mitigations: Sensitive data never in commit history
threats.residual_risk: Full repo history, IP access logs, and API usage patterns would be exposed. Private repos become visible. Anthropic API logs reveal all prompts.
threats.detection: GitHub audit log monitoring (gh api orgs/.../audit-log). Canary tokens in repo (web bugs in docs).
threats.response: Activate PEACE halt. Assess exposure scope. Rotate all credentials stored in or referenced by repos. Notify via HIVE alerts channel.

threats.name: VPS port scanning and reconnaissance
mitigations: psad on all fleet nodes for scan detection
threats.residual_risk: Sophisticated scanners using distributed/slow techniques may evade rate-based detection. Service fingerprinting on open ports reveals software versions.
threats.detection: psad alerts, portsentry triggers, iptables WG-PROBE logs, fail2ban ban events.
threats.response: Check if scan source is known (Shodan/Censys). If targeted pattern across multiple VPS: block at firewall, increase monitoring, check canaries.

threats.name: Physical location exposure from IP
threats.residual_risk: 4G carrier can geolocate SIM to cell tower. If SIM is registered, carrier links to identity. PAYG SIM mitigates but does not eliminate.
threats.detection: Verify exit IP via curl ifconfig.me after each WireGuard connection. PEACE score checks wireguard_tunnel policy.
threats.response: If home IP leaked: immediately enable VPN, audit all services accessed in exposure window, rotate API keys used during window.

threats.name: Competitor/adversary investigation
mitigations: Canary tokens planted across VPS fleet
GitHub webhook monitoring for repo events
threats.residual_risk: Determined adversary with resources can correlate business registrations, domain WHOIS (even with privacy), payment trails. Likelihood increases as Kingdom grows.
threats.detection: Canary token triggers (PDF, AWS key, web bug). Unusual GitHub activity (forks from unknown, deploy key changes). DNS canary resolution.
threats.response: Severity 1 alarm protocol. HIVE alert to all instances. Assess what canary was tripped to determine adversary's access level. Rotate exposed credentials.

threats.name: State-level surveillance
mitigations: No single point of legal access
threats.residual_risk: State actors can compel providers across jurisdictions, perform traffic analysis at scale, and deploy zero-day exploits. No practical defense against full state resources.
threats.detection: Canary warrants (if providers publish). Anomalous access patterns. Hardware anomalies on devices.
threats.response: Engage legal representation immediately. Preserve evidence. Consider full operational pause via PEACE halt. Assess whether to invoke dead-man's switch procedures.

threats.name: Ollama local model poisoning
mitigations: Pin model versions and verify checksums after pull
Run local models in sandboxed environment
Compare outputs against known-good baselines periodically
threats.residual_risk: Compromised model weights could produce subtly biased outputs, exfiltrate data through crafted responses, or inject instructions into agent pipelines. Detection of poisoned weights is an open research problem.
threats.detection: Hash verification of model blobs after each pull. Behavioral drift detection: compare outputs on test prompts against baseline. Monitor ollama server logs for unexpected network calls.
threats.response: Immediately stop using suspected model. Revert to last known-good version. Audit all outputs generated since last verified pull. Alert via HIVE.

threats.name: Kingdom-agent backend hijack via OPENAI_BASE_URL
mitigations: Validate OPENAI_BASE_URL against allowlist before agent startup
Pin base URLs in credentials/walls.json per wall level
Environment variable integrity check in PEACE score
URL allowlist in kingdom-agent.py — rejects unknown OPENAI_BASE_URL
threats.residual_risk: Attacker could modify kingdom-agent.py source to add their URL to allowlist
threats.detection: PEACE environment variable audit on each heartbeat. File integrity monitoring on .env files and shell profiles. Alert on any OPENAI_BASE_URL value not in allowlist.
threats.response: Immediate PEACE halt. Kill all running agents. Verify .env and environment variables. Rotate all API keys. Audit agent actions during compromised window.

threats.name: WireGuard tunnel key compromise
mitigations: Keys stored with restricted file permissions (600)
threats.residual_risk: Compromised WireGuard private key allows passive decryption of all tunnel traffic and active man-in-the-middle. Attacker sees all traffic routed through that tunnel.
threats.detection: Monitor for unexpected WireGuard handshakes (wg show). Alert on peer connections from unknown endpoints. PEACE fleet check for tunnel stability.
threats.response: Immediately take down compromised tunnel (wg-quick down). Generate new key pair on both ends. Re-establish tunnel. Audit all traffic that used the compromised tunnel. Rotate credentials transmitted during exposure.

threats.name: HIVE encrypted message injection
mitigations: NaCl encryption on all HIVE messages
Channel-based message routing limits blast radius
threats.residual_risk: HIVE shared key means any instance with the key can impersonate any other instance. If key is compromised, attacker can inject commands into any channel including 'alerts' and 'commands'. No per-instance authentication.
threats.detection: Message anomaly detection: unexpected sender IDs, unusual message frequency, commands from instances that should be passive. HIVE message log auditing.
threats.response: Revoke compromised HIVE key. Generate and distribute new shared key to verified instances only. Audit all messages received during exposure window. PEACE halt if command injection suspected.

threats.name: Heartbeat coordinator manipulation
mitigations: File integrity monitoring covers heartbeat scripts
Strict spawn-queue validation — only cd+kingdom-agent or cd+claude patterns allowed, dangerous patterns blocked
threats.residual_risk: Coordinator could be socially engineered to produce valid-looking but malicious commands
threats.detection: File integrity baseline checks in PEACE score. Hash verification of tick.sh and spawn-queue.sh. launchd plist integrity check. Monitor heartbeat-launchd-err.log for anomalies.
threats.response: Kill heartbeat launchd job. Verify file hashes against integrity baseline. Restore from known-good snapshot if tampered. Re-enable only after full verification. Audit all agents spawned during compromise window.

threats.name: Fleet canary false positive/negative
mitigations: Multiple canary types per node (file, DNS, web)
Cross-reference canary status with other indicators
Regular canary health verification in PEACE drill
threats.residual_risk: False positive: unnecessary incident response drains resources and causes alert fatigue. False negative: actual intrusion goes undetected because canary was disabled or bypassed by sophisticated attacker.
threats.detection: Canary health checks on each heartbeat cycle. Verify canary files exist and have expected content. Cross-correlate with fail2ban and psad data.
threats.response: For false positive: verify canary state, document root cause, update detection thresholds. For suspected false negative: assume breach, run full integrity check, deploy fresh canaries.

threats.name: DNS-over-HTTPS provider compromise
mitigations: Cloudflare DoH as primary with Quad9 as fallback
WireGuard tunnel DNS routing as secondary path
PEACE score verifies DoH is actively resolving
threats.residual_risk: Compromised DoH provider can log all DNS queries (breaking privacy promise), redirect domains to malicious IPs (DNS hijack), or selectively block resolution. Single provider dependency.
threats.detection: Periodic DNS resolution verification against multiple providers. Compare results from cloudflared vs direct resolution. PEACE doh_active check.
threats.response: Switch to backup DNS provider (Quad9). Route DNS through WireGuard tunnel instead. Verify no DNS poisoning occurred during compromise. Review domains resolved during window.

threats.name: Credential rotation during PEACE halt (recovery deadlock)
mitigations: Offline credential cache for emergency recovery
PEACE resume does not require live credential validation
threats.residual_risk: If credentials expire or are rotated while system is in PEACE halt state, the resume procedure may fail because it cannot authenticate to required services. Creates a deadlock: need credentials to resume, need system running to rotate credentials.
threats.detection: PEACE halt duration monitoring. Credential expiry tracking during halt. Pre-resume validation check.
threats.response: Use offline/cached credentials to bootstrap. Execute break-glass runbook for manual credential injection. If deadlocked: restore from snapshot with known-good credentials, then rotate.

threats.name: Session log exfiltration
mitigations: memory/sessions/ not synced to public repos
Wall system restricts session access by clearance level
Session logs restricted to 600 permissions, sessions directory 700
threats.residual_risk: Root access or process running as same user can still read
threats.detection: File access monitoring on memory/sessions/ directory. Git status check for accidental staging of session files. PEACE integrity baseline covers session directory.
threats.response: Assess what session data was exposed. Rotate any credentials or strategies referenced in exfiltrated sessions. Update operational plans that are now compromised. Consider deception: plant false session data.

threats.name: Physical device theft or seizure
mitigations: FileVault full disk encryption on all Macs
Strong login passwords unique per device
Find My Mac enabled for remote wipe
threats.residual_risk: If device is seized while unlocked or attacker obtains login password, FileVault is bypassed. Cold boot attacks on RAM. DMA attacks via Thunderbolt. Find My Mac requires internet connectivity for remote wipe.
threats.detection: Find My Mac location tracking. Heartbeat failure from missing device triggers PEACE alert. HIVE instance goes silent.
threats.response: Remote wipe via Find My Mac immediately. Rotate all credentials that were accessible on the device. Revoke SSH keys. Generate new HIVE identity. Assess wall level of lost device to scope credential rotation.

threats.name: Git history credential leak
threats.residual_risk: Accidental commit of credentials to git history is permanent even if file is later deleted. git filter-branch/BFG required for cleanup. Anyone with repo access (including via subpoena) can extract historical credentials.
threats.detection: Regular git log scan for credential patterns. PEACE file integrity check flags unexpected files in staging area. GitHub secret scanning alerts.
threats.response: Immediately rotate the exposed credential. Use BFG Repo-Cleaner to purge from history. Force push cleaned history. Audit all access to the credential during exposure window.

threats.name: Anthropic/OpenAI API key compromise
threats.residual_risk: Compromised API key allows attacker to make calls as Kingdom, potentially running up costs, accessing model capabilities, or reading API-side logs. If OPENAI_BASE_URL is also controlled (see THREAT-009), full MITM.
threats.detection: API usage monitoring for unexpected spikes. Billing alerts for cost anomalies. Provider-side access logs if available.
threats.response: Rotate compromised key immediately via credentials.py. Review API usage logs for unauthorized calls. Update all agents and configs using the old key. Notify provider if abuse detected.

threats.name: Supply chain attack on Kingdom dependencies
threats.residual_risk: Compromised pip/brew/npm package could inject backdoor into any Kingdom tool. Auto-update mechanisms bypass review. Transitive dependencies are especially opaque.
threats.detection: Monitor security advisories for used packages. Hash verification of installed binaries. Behavioral monitoring for unexpected network connections from Kingdom tools.
threats.response: Identify and remove compromised package. Audit all systems where package was installed. Rebuild affected tools from clean sources. Check for persistence mechanisms left by compromised package.
